data.title: Coinbase unveils its first credit card in partnersip with American Express
data.description:
Crypto exchange Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) will roll out if first branded credit card on the American Express (NYSE:AXP) network, it said on Thursday. The new Coinbase One Card is set to offer up to 4% bitcoin (BTC-USD) back on every purchase.

Rewards will come alongside "unique benefits, offers, experiences, and protections through the American Express Network," Will Stredwick, senior vice president, Global Network Services at American Express said on stage at Coinbase's State of Crypto summit.

The card will be exclusively available to Coinbase One members in the U.S., with the waitlist opened Thursday. Coinbase One members have mounted to over 1M since the monthly subscription product launched in 2023.

"We see real potential in the combination of Coinbase and crypto with the Powerful Backing of American Express, and what the card offers is an excellent mix of what customers are looking for right now," Stredwick said.

data.title: AngloGold rips to multiyear high on rising geopolitical risks; Roth starts coverage at Buy
data.description:
AngloGold Ashanti (NYSE:AU) +6.5% in Thursday's trading to its highest in more than 13 years, as Roth Capital initiated coverage with a Buy rating and $52 price target, citing the miner's "strong plan for growth" through both acquisitions and organic development, as well as the new dividend policy and strong gold price leverage that should make it "an attractive investment for generalist investors."

Roth analyst Joe Reagor highlighted AngloGold's (NYSE:AU) recent efforts to streamline its asset portfolio through expanding Tier 1 assets while divesting smaller operations, pointing to the company's acquisition of Centamin and its Sukari mine in November 2024, followed by the sales of its Côte d’Ivoire projects and Sierra Grande mine.

The development of its Nevada assets "should provide significant production growth" for AngloGold (AU), Reagor wrote in an analysis of the miner's future prospects.

Mining stocks are posting broad gains Thursday on heightened geopolitical risks, with President Trump saying the U.S. was moving personnel out of the Middle East because it "could be a dangerous place."

data.title: Primis Financial plans to sell a portion of Panacea stake for ~$22M
data.description:
Primis Financial (NASDAQ:FRST) announced on Thursday plans to sell a portion of its ownership in the unit, Panacea Financial Holdings, for ~$22M.

The regional lender's deconsolidation of Panacea was effective March 31. FRST had said the deconsolidation enables it to record a pre-tax gain of about $24.6M.

Primis now expects an additional gain of between $6.5M and $7.0M pre-tax as the sales price per share exceeded the carrying value as of March 31.

"Realizing this gain will allow the company to be more aggressive on certain strategies, such as its share repurchase program or accelerating growth," said CEO Dennis Zember.

data.title: Spain's Telefonica is said to eye greater role in defense investments
data.description:
Telefonica (NYSE:TEF) is aiming to expand its footprint in the defense sector as European nations ramp up military spending in response to growing geopolitical tensions, Bloomberg News reported Thursday.

Speaking before Spanish lawmakers, Chairman Marc Murtra emphasized that the telecom giant currently has only a modest presence in defense and security, a level he believes should increase. 

Telefonica already plays a crucial role as a technology and service provider to Spain’s armed forces and the Ministry of Defense. Its strategic relevance has grown following the Spanish state holding company Sepi’s move to become its largest shareholder, a shift that Murtra says positions the company to benefit from rising public sector investment in national defense.

His comments come at a time when Europe is moving to bolster its military capabilities amid ongoing concerns over Russia and uncertainty surrounding U.S. commitment to NATO. Spain alone is expected to boost its defense budget by €10.5 billion ($12.1 billion) this year, reaching NATO's 2% spending target well ahead of schedule.

Sepi took a 10% stake in Telefonica last year, in part to counterbalance Saudi Telecom’s 9.9% ownership. Other major shareholders include Criteria Caixa  and STC.

Murtra, who was appointed chairman in January by the Spanish government, is leading a strategic review of the company. A new roadmap is expected to be unveiled in the second half of 2025. In the meantime, he has repeatedly emphasized the importance of consolidation in the telecom sector and noted that Telefonica is actively exploring acquisition opportunities in Spain, Germany and the United Kingdom.

data.title: Gambling.com Group attracts a Buy rating from Texas Capital
data.description:
Texas Capital Securities initiated coverage on Gambling.com Group Ltd.(NASDAQ:GAMB) with a Buy rating.

The firm sees as Gambling.com (NASDAQ:GAMB) as the go-to affiliate/performance marketing andservices company focused on the online wagering industry. The Jersey-based company's technology and services were noted to be underpinned by visible, multi-year global online wagering total addressable market growth.

"We believe GAMB’s EBITDA will outpace industry TAM growth driven by maturation of the B2C marketing structure, an increase of brands among operators, a longer-term acceleration/mix shift of new iCasino territories, profitable entrance into whitespace jurisdictions, margin augmentation, and continued acquisition integration/execution," highlighted analyst David Bain. 

Bain and his team believe GAMB's valuation should be positively impacted by its recent acquisition of data provider Odds Holdings, which increases the recurring revenue mix and potentially helping solve a key B2C issue of hold volatility.

Looking further ahead, GAMB's business model is seen being profitably transported to significant, untapped international jurisdictions and additional M&A over the next few years is potentially in the mix.

Texas Capital's price target on Gambling.com (NASDAQ:GAMB) of $17 is based on a multiple of 10X the 2025 EV/EBITDA estimate.

Shares of Gambling.com (GAMB) are down 12.8% on a year-to-date basis. The stock has a Strong Buy rating from Seeking Alpha analysts.

data.title: AMD CEO expects AI inferencing demand to increase 80% per year
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) CEO Lisa Su said artificial intelligence inferencing demand is now outpacing training demand due to an "explosion" in the number of models.

"Training is always going to be the foundation for developing models, but the demand for inferencing has grown tremendously, driven by more capable models and increasing use cases," Su said during AMD's Advancing AI 2025 conference today in San Jose, Calif.

"We are also seeing an explosion of models, especially models for specific uses such as coding, healthcare and finance," she added. "Over the next few years we expect hundreds of thousands, and eventually millions of purpose-built models each tuned for specific tasks, industries or use cases. It drives significantly more compute, which is good for us. Agents are also adding increasing demand for GPUs. It is the equivalent of adding millions of users to the global compute infrastructure."

She said the expected demand for datacenter compute needed to power AI continues to escalate.

"When we were here last year, we said that we expected the datacenter AI accelerator TAM to grow more than 60% annually to reach $500B in 2028," Su said. "At the time, that seemed like a really big number. What I can tell you, from everything we've seen today, that number is going to be even higher. We can now see that inference inflection point. We now expect inference to grow more than 80% per year for the next few years - really becoming the largest driver of AI compute."

Su revealed the new MI350 series of GPUs, with customers such as Meta Platforms (META), Elon Musk's xAI and Microsoft (MSFT) preparing to upgrade from the MI300 to the MI350 series.

She stressed the importance of an open-source ecosytems.

"We're investing heavily in an open ecosystem," Su said. "We are really supporting every framework. We are delivering full-stack solutions. We believe an open ecosystem is essential for the future of AI ... From a portfolio standpoint, we offer the most complete suite of computing elements end to end for this vision. No matter where AI runs, or how much compute you need, AMD has the right solution. Innovation truly takes off in an open system."

"Our momentum is just accelerating," she added. "We exited the last quarter with a record 40% market share. AMD powers the two fastest supercomputers in the world. Today, seven of the top 10 model producers are using AMD Instinct in their datacenters."

Su also said the MI355, which has a 35 times performance leap from the previous series, either matches or surpasses Nvidia's (NVDA) B200 GPU when running LLMs such as DeepSeek-R1 (DEEPSEEK) or Meta's Llama 3.3 and Maverick. 

"MI355 delivers 40% more tokens per dollar than the competing GPU solution," she added.

"We are already knee-deep in the development of the MI400 series for release next year," Su said. "But today, I'm excited to launch the MI350 series. MI355 is our flagship product. It's powered by our latest fourth gen Instinct architecture. It accelerates both training and inference. Supports up to 520B parameters on a single GPU. It is also super easy to deploy the MI350 series onto existing AI datacenter infrastructure."

Eric Boyd, Microsoft's corporate vice president of AI Platform, said the high volume of memory per AMD chip provides a significant advantage when running LLMs.

data.title: Venture Global cut at UBS after recent strong performance
data.description:
Venture Global (NYSE:VG) -2.3% in Thursday's trading as UBS downgraded shares to Neutral from Buy with an $18 price target, seeing limited upside left after shares have more than doubled in the last two months vs. a mere 6.6% gain in the S&P Energy sector and dramatically outperforming peer Cheniere.

Venture Global is enjoying positive momentum in its projects, UBS analyst Manav Gupta said: The company said recently it initiated full mobilization and started site work at CP2 LNG, its third liquefied natural gas export facility, which came shortly after CP2 received final approval and notices to proceed from the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission on the project, and its Plaquemines LNG project received FERC approval to introduce natural gas to its 12th liquefaction block, meaning the developer now has approval to flow gas to more than half of the full project, which will have nameplate LNG production capacity of 20M metric tons/year.

Gupta noted Venture Global's (NYSE:VG) modular approach to development, which allows for speed to market so the first LNG can be achieved in 2.5-3 years post FID compared to the traditional approach with long lead times of 5-7 years; the company starts marketing these cargoes during a long commissioning phase that can last up to 30 months, allowing it to capture the higher net spreads of global gas price vs. Henry Hub, which lowers overall project development costs, which are passed to its long-term customers.

data.title: Bitcoin is the new 'refuge' as U.S. debt grows exponentially, Coinbase CEO says
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) is emerging as a "refuge" as concerns about ballooning U.S. debt and persistent inflation drive more investors to the cryptocurrency, Coinbase Global (NASDAQ:COIN) CEO Brian Armstrong said on Thursday.

"People are turning to bitcoin as a refuge from inflation and deficit spending," Armstrong said in a presentation at a Coinbase event.

"It's not a coincidence that we're seeing all-time high prices of bitcoin (BTC-USD)," he added. If the exponential growth in U.S. federal debt continues, "there's a very good chance bitcoin becomes" the next global reserve currency.

In the meantime, Armstrong also made several product announcements as crypto becomes more mainstream. He introduced a new Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) product for startups and medium-sized businesses; a new set of payment APIs that bridge the gap between stablecoins and fiat currencies; introducing derivatives trading and integrating decentralized exchange trading ("DEX"); and building a new consumer payment product.

Earlier at the same event, U.S. President Donald Trump's crypto czar David Sacks said "we're well on our way" to making the U.S. the global center for digital asset innovation, reversing a tough and unclear regulatory environment under the Biden administration.

data.title: Nurix drops after new early-stage trial data for lead asset
data.description:
Shares of Nurix Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NRIX) fell on Thursday after the company updated data from a Phase 1a/b clinical trial for its lead asset bexobrutideg (NX-5948) in patients with relapsed or refractory B-cell malignancies.

The data had been selected for a presentation at the ongoing European Hematology Association Congress.

According to Nurix (NASDAQ:NRIX), its Bruton’s tyrosine kinase degrader achieved an overall objective response rate of 80.9% among 47 patients with relapsed or refractory chronic lymphocytic leukemia.

Citing data from 19 patients, the company added that those with Waldenström Macroglobulinemia witnessed an overall objective response rate of 84.2%.

The analysis was based on a data cut on March 12 when the median follow-up period had approached nine months.

The study, designed to test oral bexobrutide doses ranging from 50 mg to 600 mg, indicated a well-tolerated safety profile for the drug with no safety signals, the company noted. Nurix (NASDAQ:NRIX) reaffirmed its plans to begin pivotal trials for bexobrutideg this year.

More on Nurix

  • Nurix Therapeutics: A Story To Monitor
  • Nurix Therapeutics: Speculative Bet On BTK Degraders And Targeted Protein Modulation
  • Nurix gets FDA clearance to initiate trial for its anti-inflammatory drug candidate
  • Nurix Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.67 beats by $0.11, revenue of $18.45M beats by $5.42M

data.title: Frito-Lay shutting down production at Southern California plant
data.description:
• Frito-Lay (NASDAQ:PEP) is shutting down production at its Rancho Cucamonga plant in Southern California after 50 years in operation.
  • Although no reason was given for the decision, Frito-Lay North America ("FLNA"), a division of PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) reported an 11% decline in operating profit in Q4 2024, primarily reflecting operating cost increases and a decline in organic volume.
  • The company no longer strips out Frito-Lay North America financial results. The division is now included with Quaker Foods into a single unit, PepsiCo Foods North America ("PFNA"). 
  • Although the company will maintain its distribution, fleet, and warehouse operations at the Rancho Cucamonga plant, shutting down manufacturing is expected to result in the loss of “hundreds” of jobs, according to USA Today.
  • Because the Rancho Cucamonga plant is not registered with California’s Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) database -- which requires employers to give 60 days’ notice before layoffs – the timing of the closure and the actual number of employees impacted is unknown.
